varieties.name: Mammoth
varieties.notes: Single-stem giant reaching 10-12 ft. Produces one large head per stalk; excellent for seed harvest. Sow after last frost when soil is at least 50 °F.

varieties.name: Teddy Bear
varieties.notes: Dwarf variety (2-3 ft) with fully double, fluffy golden blooms. Great for containers and borders. Does not transplant well due to taproot.

varieties.name: Autumn Beauty
varieties.notes: Multi-branching variety (5-7 ft) with blooms in red, bronze, yellow, and bicolor shades. Produces multiple cut flowers per plant over a longer period than single-stem types.

varieties.name: Giant Dahlia
varieties.notes: Large 4-6 in. dahlia-form blooms on 3-4 ft stems in a wide color range. Excellent cut flower with long vase life. Pinch at 12 in. to encourage branching.

varieties.name: Profusion
varieties.notes: Compact mounding habit (12-18 in.) with prolific single blooms. Outstanding disease resistance to powdery mildew. Self-cleaning; no deadheading needed for continuous bloom.

varieties.name: State Fair
varieties.notes: Tall variety (3-4 ft) with massive 5 in. double blooms. Classic mix includes scarlet, orange, yellow, pink, lavender, and white. Prefers full sun and good air circulation.

varieties.name: French
varieties.notes: Compact plants (6-12 in.) with double crested blooms in yellow, orange, and red bicolors. Excellent border and companion plant; may deter some garden pests. Deadhead regularly for continuous bloom.

varieties.name: African
varieties.notes: Tall plants (2-3 ft) bearing large 3-5 in. pom-pom blooms in gold, yellow, and orange. Start indoors 4-6 weeks before last frost for longest bloom season. Heat and drought tolerant once established.

varieties.name: Sensation
varieties.notes: Tall airy plants (3-5 ft) with large daisy-like blooms in white, pink, and crimson. Thrives in poor to average soil; over-fertilizing reduces flowering. Self-sows readily in mild climates.

varieties.name: Chocolate
varieties.notes: Deep maroon-brown single blooms with a true chocolate fragrance. Grows 2-3 ft tall. Not as vigorous as Sensation; benefits from consistent moisture. Excellent specialty cut flower.

varieties.name: Jewel Mix
varieties.notes: Compact bushy habit (12-15 in.) with semi-double blooms in scarlet, gold, orange, and cherry. Edible flowers and leaves with a peppery flavor. Prefers poor soil; rich soil promotes foliage over blooms. Does not transplant well.
